nodes.x: I said, I will take heed to my ways, that I sin not with my tongue: I will keep my mouth with a bridle, while the wicked is before me.
Behold I the Lord utter my voice, and it ahal[ be obeyed. Wherefore verily T say, let the wicked take heed, and let the rebellious fear
My lips shall not speak wickedness, nor my tongue utter deceit.
For my mouth shall speak truth; and wickedness is an abomination to my lips.

Wherefore, verily I say, let the wicked take heed, and let the rebellious fear and tremble; and let the unbelieving hold their lips, for the day of wrath shall come upon them as a whirlwind, and all flesh shall know that I am God.
Behold, I am God; give heed unto my word, which is quick and powerful, sharper than a two-edged sword, to the dividing asunder of both joints and marrow; therefore give heed unto my words.
I was dumb with silence, I held my peace, even from good; and my sorrow was stirred.
And when he had spoken such words unto me, I set my face toward the ground, and I became dumb.
Unto me men gave ear, and waited, and kept silence at my counsel.
Unless the Lord had been my help, my soul had almost dwelt in silence.

For a few moments after the song was finished I sat overpowered with a
feeling of indescribable peace. When I looked up the room was empty, my
friends had gone.

How often has my mind been filled with light, and my bosom with joy, while I have thus poured out my soul, in pourtraying the blessings which pertain to the citizens of the kingdom — and as I thought had made the path of entrance so plain that “the wayfaring man, though a fool, might not err therein when every nerve of my body had been exhausted, and my voice had almost lost its tone : when…
My heart was hot within me, while I was musing the fire burned: then spake I with my tongue,
Thus my heart was grieved, and I was pricked in my reins.
"When three years of age, I fell into a caldron of scalding water and although instantly rescued, I was so badly burned
The Spirit of the Lord spake by me, and his word was in my tongue.
My heart is sore pained within me: and the terrors of death are fallen upon me.
Lord, make me to know mine end, and the measure of my days, what it is; that I may know how frail I am.
That I have great heaviness and continual sorrow in my heart.
And the day cometh that you shall hear my voice and see me, and know that I am.

And now that my soul might have joy in you, and that my heart might leave this world with gladness because of you, that I might not be brought down with grief and sorrow to the grave, arise from the dust, my sons, and be men, and be determined in one mind and in one heart, united in all things, that ye may not come down into captivity;
That all the ends of the earth may know that we, thy servants, have heard thy voice, and that thou hast sent us;
That the fulness of my gospel might be proclaimed by the weak and the simple unto the ends of the world, and before kings and rulers.
Behold, thou hast made my days as an handbreadth; and mine age is as nothing before thee: verily every man at his best state is altogether vanity. Selah.
32 And so perisheth every young man, darkening his mind from the truth, and not understanding the law of God, nor obeying the admonitions of his fathers, as befell me also in my youth.

to its paradisiacal state. But we must first redeem ourselves from every root of bitterness that may be in our nature, striving daily to overcome the evil that is in the world and in ourselves, sanctifying our hearts and affections until there shall be nothing abiding in us contrary to the Holy Ghost in its perfect and full fruition of enjoyment to the creature. I think it likely that after a…
Remember now thy Creator in the days of thy youth, while the evil days come not, nor the years draw nigh, when thou shalt say, I have no pleasure in them;

Brethren and sisters—I feel it a privilege to have a part in the work of the latter days, and I feel thankful that I have been brought upon the stage of existence in this period of time. It is a privilege which you all enjoy as well as myself, and one which we should appreciate more than we do. When we reflect upon the misery and degradation that prevail in the world, we ought to highly prize the…
Surely every man walketh in a vain shew: surely they are disquieted in vain: he heapeth up riches, and knoweth not who shall gather them.
And again, The Lord knoweth the thoughts of the wise, that they are vain.
For he knoweth vain men: he seeth wickedness also; will he not then consider it?
Seek not after riches nor the vain things of this world; for behold, you cannot carry them with you.
Remember how short my time is: wherefore hast thou made all men in vain?
And turn ye not aside: for then should ye go after vain things, which cannot profit nor deliver; for they are vain.
And now, Lord, what wait I for? my hope is in thee.
For in thee, O Lord, do I hope: thou wilt hear, O Lord my God.
But I will hope continually, and will yet praise thee more and more.
Let thy mercy, O Lord, be upon us, according as we hope in thee.
I wait for the Lord, my soul doth wait, and in his word do I hope.

And what is it that ye shall hope for? Behold I say unto you that ye shall have hope through the atonement of Christ and the power of his resurrection, to be raised unto life eternal, and this because of your faith in him according to the promise.
Deliver me from all my transgressions: make me not the reproach of the foolish.
Turn away my reproach which I fear: for thy judgments are good.
Deliver me from mine enemies, O my God: defend me from them that rise up against me.
Behold, I send you out to reprove the world of all their unrighteous deeds, and to teach them of a judgment which is to come.

'I write all this to you, my dearest friend, to forewarn you of my
silly tastes; and, at all events, that I may put it in your power to
take some preparatory steps, in one place or another, for my
settlement. My demands are, in truth, confoundedly naive, but your
goodness has spoiled me.
For I acknowledge my transgressions: and my sin is ever before me.
I was dumb, I opened not my mouth; because thou didst it.
Now the hand of the Lord was upon me in the evening, afore he that was escaped came; and had opened my mouth, until he came to me in the morning; and my mouth was opened, and I was no more dumb.
But I, as a deaf man, heard not; and I was as a dumb man that openeth not his mouth.
Behold, now I have opened my mouth, my tongue hath spoken in my mouth.
With my lips have I declared all the judgments of thy mouth.
Remove thy stroke away from me: I am consumed by the blow of thine hand.
Withdraw thine hand far from me: and let not thy dread make me afraid.
Thine hands have made me and fashioned me together round about; yet thou dost destroy me.
Why withdrawest thou thy hand, even thy right hand? pluck it out of thy bosom.
Send thine hand from above; rid me, and deliver me out of great waters, from the hand of strange children;
For thine arrows stick fast in me, and thy hand presseth me sore.
When thou with rebukes dost correct man for iniquity, thou makest his beauty to consume away like a moth: surely every man is vanity. Selah.
He that soweth iniquity shall reap vanity: and the rod of his anger shall fail.
And behold, if the Lord shall say unto a man—Because of thine iniquities, thou shalt be accursed forever—it shall be done.
And again, because iniquity shall abound, the love of men shall wax cold; but he that shall not be overcome, the same shall be saved.
Therefore every one that eateth it shall bear his iniquity, because he hath profaned the hallowed thing of the Lord: and that soul shall be cut off from among his people.
Thou hast forgiven the iniquity of thy people, thou hast covered all their sin. Selah.
Hear my prayer, O Lord, and give ear unto my cry; hold not thy peace at my tears: for I am a stranger with thee, and a sojourner, as all my fathers were.
Hear my prayer, O Lord, and let my cry come unto thee.
Have respect therefore to the prayer of thy servant, and to his supplication, O Lord my God, to hearken unto the cry and the prayer which thy servant prayeth before thee:
Hearken unto the voice of my cry, my King, and my God: for unto thee will I pray.
Yet have thou respect unto the prayer of thy servant, and to his supplication, O Lord my God, to hearken unto the cry and to the prayer, which thy servant prayeth before thee to day:
Lord, I cry unto thee: make haste unto me; give ear unto my voice, when I cry unto thee.
O spare me, that I may recover strength, before I go hence, and be no more.
What is my strength, that I should hope? and what is mine end, that I should prolong my life?
Cast me not off in the time of old age; forsake me not when my strength faileth.
I can do all things through Christ which strengtheneth me.
My strength is dried up like a potsherd; and my tongue cleaveth to my jaws; and thou hast brought me into the dust of death.
